user_query:
I need a small, reproducible knowledge snapshot for a few core biology entities for my lab notes. Please use the identifiers in input/targets.csv to fetch each entity’s official entry page from the correct source, save the raw HTML, and extract some basic fields into a structured summary.

Sources and URL patterns to construct the official pages (no search engines; build URLs directly):
- NCBI Gene: on the ncbi.nlm.nih.gov domain under the Gene section at path "/gene/{GeneID}" using the numeric GeneID
- UniProtKB: on the uniprot.org domain under the UniProtKB entry path "/uniprotkb/{Accession}" using the accession ID
- Gene Ontology (GO) AmiGO: on the amigo.geneontology.org domain under the path "/amigo/term/{GO ID}" using the GO identifier (e.g., "GO:0006915")

Requirements:
1) For each row in input/targets.csv, construct the page URL using the above pattern for its source and identifier. Download the page HTML and save it to data/raw/{source}/{identifier}.html (e.g., data/raw/NCBI_Gene/7157.html). Create parent directories as needed.
2) Handle errors robustly: capture the downloader’s HTTP status and any stderr/stdout error messages. If a download fails or returns a non-2xx status, retry once; if it still fails, record the failure and move on. Analyze the command output (or exception) to populate status and error details.
3) For each successfully downloaded HTML file, extract these fields:
   - title_text: the document title from the <title> element
   - heading_text: the first main heading text (prefer an <h1>; if none, fall back to <h2>)
   - meta_description: the content from a <meta name="description"> or <meta property="og:description"> if present
   - summary_text: the first visible paragraph of the main content if readily accessible; if not, fall back to meta_description; if neither is available, leave null
   Also compute html_size_bytes (file size in bytes) and html_sha256 (SHA-256 hash of the raw HTML). For summary_text, also include summary_char_len (character length; 0 if null).
4) Write one JSON Lines file at outputs/extract/summary.jsonl where each line is an object with this schema:
   {"source": str, "identifier": str, "url_used": str, "download_status": "ok"|"error", "http_status": int|null, "html_size_bytes": int, "html_sha256": str|null, "title_text": str|null, "heading_text": str|null, "meta_description": str|null, "summary_text": str|null, "summary_char_len": int, "error_message": str|null}
   - For failures, set download_status="error", html_size_bytes=0, html_sha256=null, content fields to null, and include the best available error_message from the downloader output.
5) Save a plain-text log of all download attempts (including command output and errors) at logs/download.log.
6) Write a short methods note at outputs/METHODS.md describing: (a) how you constructed URLs from the identifiers, (b) how you detected and handled non-200 responses and errors, and (c) a brief summary of how many succeeded vs failed.

Deliverables to create:
- Raw HTML files under data/raw/{source}/{identifier}.html for each input row (present when downloads succeed)
- logs/download.log capturing command outputs and error messages
- outputs/extract/summary.jsonl with one record per input row following the schema above
- outputs/METHODS.md (brief text as described)

Please make sure the process does not rely on interactive steps and that failures are recorded rather than stopping the run. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
